questions.question: What is the purpose of an MVP?
questions.expectedAnswer: To test the riskiest assumption with the smallest possible investment. An MVP is an experiment, not a feature-stripped product. It answers: 'Is this worth building?' before committing to full development.
questions.rubric: Must frame MVP as an experiment/learning tool, not a small product

questions.question: A stakeholder asks for a feature and says 'I need this by Friday.' How do you respond as a product manager?
questions.expectedAnswer: Understand the underlying need (why Friday? what problem does the feature solve?). Evaluate against current priorities. If it conflicts with higher-priority work, communicate the trade-off. Negotiate scope if timeline is fixed. Never promise what the team cannot deliver.
questions.rubric: Must show problem-first thinking and trade-off communication
